channel.title: Foundation Universe (new posts)
channel.description: Posts in the forum category "Foundation Universe" - Discussion of things relating to both the SCP objects and the "universe" of the Foundation as a whole.

item.title: Finding the Scarlet King's children.: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded: Where is this said?

item.title: Reality Benders, Hoppers, and Dimension crossing.: Re: Reality Benders, Hoppers, and Dimension crossing.
item.content:encoded: A device that reaches into the either the brains or the entire body of the reality bender, stabling them to the machine so anywhere the reality bender goes, the machine goes also?

item.title: What are your unusual headcanons?: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: The numbering of the SCPs is chosen by the O5/Administrator and has a special in-universe meaning. That's why some old SCP have larger numbers and some SCP that can't exist in some canon doesn't have two entries.

item.title: Does Spicy Crust Pizza have an email address?: Does Spicy Crust Pizza have an email address?
item.content:encoded: I'm making a skip and it involves the Foundation Front company, Spicy Crust Pizza. I'm wondering if that company has an official email in-universe. If there is one, do you know what the name of it is?

item.title: Hearts and minds?: Re: Hearts and minds?
item.content:encoded: Oh, yeah, absolutely. The Foundation doesn’t care one bit about getting humanity’s approval/blessing/permission for anything. If they had their way they’d work totally independent of national entities or the UN to get the job done on their own terms.

item.title: Hearts and minds?: Re: Hearts and minds?
item.content:encoded: Oh, I apologize, I didn't mean to be reductive. When I said they don't care about hearts in mind, I meant in the usual sense of gaining the confidence of the people you're protecting. They obviously don't need to worry about that, since their primary goal is secrecy.

item.title: What makes the Foundation the Foundation?: Re: What makes the Foundation the Foundation?
item.content:encoded:
No problem! Yeah, people have a lot of different views off the Foundation. I think some people headcanon that each SCP's research team is super-isolated and nobody even discusses their SCP with anyone else due to security. That doesn't make sense to me, but to each their own.

They probably would look like how you described them as an academic place.

That reminds me of this tale, where I think something almost exactly like that happened. (It's both Broken Masquerade and the Lethe doomsday canon, and I think the idea is that people forgot about the Foundation.)

they most likely store it but it made me think that there is a possibility that they give it to humanity to improve the condition of the world but that depends on how you look at the foundation. thank you for responding!

That's also what I believe: things get designated as explained (-EX) and then potentially slowly leaked to the public. I think we just don't have many articles showing that on it wiki because they'd most likely be boring and hard to write.

item.title: What makes the Foundation the Foundation?: Re: What makes the Foundation the Foundation?
item.content:encoded:
that's an interesting take on the foundation and I can definitely see them as an academic place of study. What would the foundation be without the core belief Secure, Contain, Protect? They probably would look like how you described them as an academic place. People view the foundation differently in most of the things I read on the wiki and from responses in previous posts. You also gave me an idea, what happens to an anomalous item once the foundation knows how it works?
they most likely store it but it made me think that there is a possibility that they give it to humanity to improve the condition of the world but that depends on how you look at the foundation. thank you for responding!

item.title: What makes the Foundation the Foundation?: Re: What makes the Foundation the Foundation?
item.content:encoded:
thanks for your response!

so the sites would still operate normally without the researchers and the council? How would the look like and would anything change because of it? Could the Foundation work on a global scale from just one site location? could a small team of researchers and a quarter of the council, along with the staff of one site keep the anomalies at bay? For the foundation's essence of research and containment would it prevent them from working with any anomalous group or would a similar group with the foundation's core interests would be able to work with them?

item.title: Differences between MTF Omega-7 and MTF Alpha-9: Re: Differences between MTF Omega-7 and MTF Alpha-9
item.content:encoded:
I don't know the current state of the Resurrection canon, but I'm pretty sure they aren't actually fully approved and in action in that canon.

Alpha-9 was approved in-fiction in The High Court With The Magic Army and first put into action during Operation "Camp Granada".

item.title: Differences between MTF Omega-7 and MTF Alpha-9: Re: Differences between MTF Omega-7 and MTF Alpha-9
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much! I'll try to keep all this in mind while I write.

item.title: Differences between MTF Omega-7 and MTF Alpha-9: Re: Differences between MTF Omega-7 and MTF Alpha-9
item.content:encoded:
Omega-7 was a much earlier version of the team that was disbanded after SCP-076 (who was a member) went berserk and an entire Site was nuked to stop him. I know you said you didn't want to read details, but the story is here. It's a lot shorter than the Alpha-9 stuff.

Alpha-9 comes from the Resurrection canon, where the idea of an anomalous humanoid SCP MTF was, well, resurrected. SCP-105 is the only member of Omega-7 who is also in Alpha-9. I don't know the current state of the Resurrection canon, but I'm pretty sure they aren't actually fully approved and in action in that canon.

A word to the wise: Outside of the fiction, most SCPs that were in Omega-7 or were written to be in Omega-7 were deleted due to low quality. If you're thinking about writing a humanoid SCP into an MTF, be careful, you're heading into rough waters.

Honestly, most of the anomalous humanoids I know of in MTFs have their own specific MTFs instead of being in Alpha-9, and even then you still need to justify it. For example, Tau-5 sees a lot of use beyond their original stories, whereas I'm pretty sure Alpha-9 is only talked about in that particular canon.

item.title: Differences between MTF Omega-7 and MTF Alpha-9: Differences between MTF Omega-7 and MTF Alpha-9
item.content:encoded: I've been working on an idea for an SCP article and while trying to brainstorm ideas for it I looked at the MTF squads and noticed Omega-7 and Alpha-9. I don't really have the time or the will to read through both of these and everything about them, so could someone give me a rundown on the two? Like what's the differences between the two, how they came about, who's invovled, and most importantly would Last Hope be where ALL humaniod SCP's the Foundation is trying to use in the field or just some?

item.title: Reality Benders, Hoppers, and Dimension crossing.: Re: Reality Benders, Hoppers, and Dimension crossing.
item.content:encoded: I suppose that's possible in the sense that you can write it, yeah. I'm not sure how solid the justification behind that science would be — "dimensional experiment machines"? — but it's certainly possible to write it.

item.title: Hearts and minds?: Re: Hearts and minds?
item.content:encoded:
Now, I know the obvious answer is no, they don't care about hearts and minds.

I feel like this is an oversimplification of the Foundation's worldview, honestly — they do have a strong sense of morality, but they're pathologically obsessed with injustices being done for the greater good. The atrocious acts you allude to aren't really done unthinkingly; presumably, someone at the top had to submit a request to the Ethics Committee, who weighed the risk and the reward and the necessity, and then gave the okay.

On an individual level, that's why you see personnel reacting so violently to things like Montauk, since they recognize the obvious inhumanity of it but also accept that their higher-ups have decided that it's for humanity's best. I imagine the same process would play out in your hypothetical retrieval mission, where it depends on someone in the chain of command weighing the risks and giving the order.

item.title: Reality Benders, Hoppers, and Dimension crossing.: Re: Reality Benders, Hoppers, and Dimension crossing.
item.content:encoded:
stick em in dimensional experiment machines and use them as a form of a dimensional energy

I'm pretty sure the foundation could but the ethics committee wouldn't approve it, unless it was in the early days of the foundation like how they used The Children in the ouroboros cycle

item.title: Information on the IS and IS NOT entity from SCP-4840?: Re: Information on the IS and IS NOT entity from SCP-4840?
item.content:encoded:
I'd hazard a guess that IS and IS NOT were two questions: "What is?" and "What isn't?"

i like this idea

On Eve, She could be out their somewhere, but that's for other writers to figure out.

this might sound cheezy but i would guess eve may be Earth itself, mother gaia so to speak

item.title: Information on the IS and IS NOT entity from SCP-4840?: Re: Information on the IS and IS NOT entity from SCP-4840?
item.content:encoded: nice, you actually have the same interpretation I have of them. I actually intended on writing an scp of someone who had died and transported to the edge of the universe and meets IS NOT. to me IS NOT is the space of nonexistence that the expansion of the big bang haven't reach

item.title: Reality Benders, Hoppers, and Dimension crossing.: Reality Benders, Hoppers, and Dimension crossing.
item.content:encoded:
We did believe reality benders can warp anything they live respectively of the universe/dimension they reside in, but i never think they could achieve crossing dimensions from the thought of anomalous limits. 507 is not a reality bender since he has just a displacement effect yet to my question:

is it possible you can use anomaly people like him along with reality benders, stick em in dimensional experiment machines and use them as a form of a dimensional energy to power a vehicle that can cross through the other ever expanding dimensional worlds?

item.title: The Leak: Re: The Leak
item.content:encoded:
Almost certain this has been asked before, but what does 096 do to its victims? I have my own theory about what happens and why it needs to be kept secret, but I'm interested to see if there are any accepted answers.

Edit: literally just found the comment chain, please disregard.
